narrative:
Dayot Upamecano is a key Centre-Back for France. His overall rating of 7.41/10 places him in the top 1% of centre-backs at this tournament. This high rating, with 77% from club form, highlights his elite status heading into the latter stages.

Upamecano's defensive prowess is evident from his World Cup statistics across 436 minutes played. He averages 2.477 interceptions and 3.716 clearances per 90 minutes, demonstrating his ability to break up play and relieve pressure. His offensive contribution is minimal, with just 0.027 expected goals and 0.086 expected assists per 90 minutes.

Upamecano clearly serves as a defensive anchor for his national team, focusing on disrupting opposition attacks rather than creating his own. Scouts should continue to monitor his impressive 2.271 tackles and 2.477 interceptions per 90 minutes. His ability to maintain this elite defensive output will be crucial for France in the tournament's decisive matches.
